title: Project workspace setup
acceptance: Repository has root scripts for typecheck and tests
edgeCases: Empty repo with no prior commits

title: NestJS API scaffold
acceptance: NestJS app boots in test mode
Health endpoint is covered by a failing-first test

title: Shared TypeScript core package
acceptance: Core package exports public domain types
No app imports private implementation paths

title: Postgres schema and migration setup
acceptance: Initial schema covers tenant and audit foundations

title: CI pipeline with typecheck tests lint and migration checks
acceptance: CI runs typecheck, tests, lint, and migration checks

title: React Flow visual builder
acceptance: Users can add, move, connect, and delete nodes
Core graph operations are unit tested

title: Agent role nodes
acceptance: Role node captures instructions, language policy, and default model tier

title: Tool nodes
acceptance: Tool node binds to a permitted integration tool
Risk and approval state are visible

title: Handoff nodes
acceptance: Handoff node targets a valid specialist

title: Human escalation nodes
acceptance: Escalation node binds to a queue

title: Workflow validation
acceptance: Validator catches missing entry, unreachable nodes, unsafe cycles, and missing tool auth

title: Version publishing
acceptance: Calls pin to a published version
Draft changes do not affect active calls

title: Runtime manifest preview
acceptance: Users can preview compiled manifest before publish
Preview includes runtime, telephony, memory, tools, and budget

title: Runtime manifest compiler
acceptance: Compiler converts published workflow to manifest

title: Cost optimized sandwich runtime adapter
acceptance: Adapter streams STT to text model to TTS

title: Balanced runtime profile
acceptance: Balanced profile uses stronger routing and TTS options

title: Premium OpenAI Realtime profile
acceptance: Premium profile is opt-in by policy
Tool and handoff events are observed

title: Model routing policy engine
acceptance: Rules select tiers by intent, risk, confidence, language, and call phase

title: Runtime budget and cost estimation
acceptance: Estimate includes telephony, STT, model, TTS, and storage
Tenant budgets can block publish or call start

title: Sandbox call session
acceptance: Browser sandbox starts a test call

title: Telephony connection model
acceptance: Model supports platform managed, BYO SIP, and BYO provider account

title: Platform managed telephony connection
acceptance: Platform numbers can map to agent versions

title: BYO SIP trunk connection
acceptance: Tenant can configure SIP trunk details

title: Twilio number import and routing
acceptance: Imported numbers map to published versions

title: Inbound call dispatch
acceptance: Inbound call resolves tenant and published version

title: Outbound call dispatch
acceptance: Outbound calls enforce consent, budget, and calling window

title: Call recording policy
acceptance: Recording can be disabled by tenant/workflow

title: DTMF voicemail transfer and failover handling
acceptance: DTMF, voicemail, transfer, and failover are first-class events
Edge cases are covered by tests

title: Provider health checks and test calls
acceptance: Health checks run for each provider connection
Failures block production routing when required

title: OAuth connection framework
acceptance: Platform OAuth apps support connect and callback

title: Encrypted credential storage
acceptance: Tokens and provider secrets are encrypted at rest
No raw secrets are returned from APIs

title: HubSpot connector
acceptance: Connector can look up contacts and write notes

title: Google Workspace connector
acceptance: Connector can read calendar availability and create events

title: Notion connector
acceptance: Connector can search knowledge and create pages/tasks

title: Webhook HTTP tool connector
acceptance: Tenant can define HTTP tool schema
Timeout and retry policy are enforced

title: Tool permission grants
acceptance: Tools require explicit grants by role/workflow

title: Session memory
acceptance: Active call memory is available within the session
Session memory is cleared or summarized after call

title: Tenant knowledge memory
acceptance: Tenant knowledge can store policies and FAQs

title: pgvector retrieval
acceptance: Embeddings are stored in Postgres pgvector
Top-k retrieval has scope and confidence filters

title: Memory approval workflow
acceptance: Tenant can require approval before durable memory write

title: Memory edit delete UI API
acceptance: Users can view, edit, delete, and disable memory

title: Knowledge ingestion pipeline
acceptance: Pipeline ingests docs, websites, PDFs, Notion, Google Drive, and CRM help centers

title: Memory privacy and retention enforcement
acceptance: Retention policies purge memory and sources

title: Live call monitor
acceptance: Operators see active calls, agent role, runtime tier, and status

title: Transcript and event timeline
acceptance: Timeline shows transcript, tools, handoffs, routing, and errors
Events can be replayed after call

title: Model tool cost telemetry
acceptance: Telemetry captures model, tool, latency, and cost
Metrics aggregate by tenant and call

title: Escalation queue
acceptance: Escalations enter queue with reason and SLA

title: Human takeover callback fallback
acceptance: Takeover or callback fallback follows provider capability

title: Post-call summary
acceptance: Summary includes outcome, action items, and disposition

title: Quality flags and improvement suggestions
acceptance: System flags dead ends, hallucinations, slow turns, and escalation misses

title: Tenant isolation tests
acceptance: Automated tests prove tenant data isolation

title: Audit logging
acceptance: Records include actor, tenant, target, and timestamp
Audit logs are immutable enough for v1

title: Call consent and recording notices
acceptance: Notices play before recording where required

title: Retention and deletion workflows
acceptance: Tenant retention policies apply to calls, transcripts, memory, and recordings

title: Secrets encryption and key rotation metadata
Prompt injection defenses
acceptance: Tool outputs and knowledge are treated as untrusted
System instructions are separated from retrieved content

title: Outbound abuse rate limits
acceptance: Outbound calls enforce rate limits and consent

title: Do-not-call and timezone safe calling windows
Redaction pipeline
acceptance: PII/sensitive data redaction runs before storage where configured

title: General SaaS compliance readiness
acceptance: Readiness checklist covers encryption, audit, retention, consent, and access control

title: Usage metering
acceptance: Usage aggregates by tenant and feature

title: Telephony minute accounting
acceptance: Minutes are computed by provider connection and tenant

title: Model STT TTS cost accounting
acceptance: Model/STT/TTS usage maps to runtime events

title: Plan limits and tenant budgets
acceptance: Tenant budgets can cap calls and premium runtime use

title: Production deployment plan
acceptance: Production environment, release process, secrets, migrations, and rollback are documented

title: Observability dashboards
acceptance: Dashboards cover calls, latency, errors, cost, integrations, and telephony

title: Backup and disaster recovery
acceptance: Backups cover DB and critical object storage

title: Provider outage fallback
acceptance: Fallback routes exist for telephony/runtime providers
Calls fail safely when no fallback exists

title: Final production readiness checklist
acceptance: Checklist covers tests, docs, security, compliance, billing, observability, and rollback

title: Frontend auth client setup
acceptance: Better Auth React client is configured for both Vite apps
Login, logout, and session state work against the NestJS auth backend
Route guards cover unauthenticated, tenant, and platform-admin users
edgeCases: Session expires while app is open

title: Platform role and permission model
acceptance: Shared platform and tenant role types exist
NestJS guards distinguish platform roles from tenant roles
Tests prove tenant admins are not platform admins

title: Platform admin app scaffold
acceptance: It has independent routing, shell, build script, and env config
It shares only approved packages with tenant app

title: Platform admin auth client and access gate
acceptance: Platform admin app uses Better Auth React client
Non-platform users are blocked from admin UI
Server-side platform guard rejects unauthorized API calls

title: Platform admin dashboard shell
acceptance: Dashboard shows system health, tenants, calls, runtime status, spend, incidents, and abuse queues
Navigation is independent from tenant app
UI smoke test covers dashboard load

title: Platform organization management
acceptance: Platform admins can view tenant status, plan, usage, telephony, integration state, and risk flags

title: Platform user and membership support tools
acceptance: Platform admins can view users and memberships
Support actions are permissioned and audited
No raw secrets or credentials are exposed

title: Platform telephony operations dashboard
acceptance: Platform admins can inspect platform-managed, BYO SIP, and BYO Twilio connections
Health, route, and webhook failures are visible
Raw provider credentials are never exposed

title: Platform integration operations dashboard
acceptance: Platform admins can inspect connector health, token status, sync failures, and revocation state
Raw OAuth tokens are never exposed

title: Runtime provider health dashboard
acceptance: Platform admins can see STT, TTS, model, realtime, telephony, and queue health by provider and region
Health events include timestamps and severity

title: Platform usage and billing controls
acceptance: Platform admins can inspect usage, budgets, overages, premium realtime usage, and plan limits across tenants
Readonly admins cannot mutate billing controls

title: Platform admin audit log
acceptance: Every platform admin action records actor, target, tenant, action, timestamp, metadata, and impersonation state
Audit log can be filtered by actor, tenant, and action
Audit records are not editable by normal admins

title: Platform impersonation workflow
acceptance: Impersonation is time-boxed, permissioned, visibly marked, auditable, and revocable
Destructive actions are blocked unless explicitly allowed
Tenant and platform audit records link to the impersonation session

title: Abuse and compliance review queue
acceptance: Platform admins can review outbound abuse signals, DNC violations, consent issues, prompt-injection flags, and suspension recommendations
Queue supports safe escalation and dismissal

title: Platform admin deployment and domain config
acceptance: `apps/platform-admin` has separate deploy config and environment variables
Trusted origins include local, staging, and production admin domains
Security headers and CSP can differ from tenant app
edgeCases: Wrong domain points to tenant app

title: Shared frontend packages setup
acceptance: `packages/ui`, `packages/api-client`, and `packages/auth-client` are planned or scaffolded for shared frontend code
Shared packages do not depend on tenant-only or admin-only app code
edgeCases: Admin-only component leaks into tenant app
